$schema-note: The justified, content-addressed equivalent-mutant registry (Slice C). Each entry marks a RUNTIME mutant the deterministic engine cannot statically exclude but that is PROVABLY behaviour-identical to the original, so no test could ever kill it. The ONLY honest way to exclude such a mutant — NEVER a fake test. Matched by `mutantId` (the blake3 content address of {file,operator,line,column,originalText,mutatedText}); the file/line/operator/rewrite fields are advisory human-review provenance. ANTI-DRIFT: if the code changes the id changes and the entry no longer matches, re-surfacing the mutant as a normal survivor. Owner-redlinable.

entries.justification: The key-sort comparator `left < right` orders the entries of Object.entries(value). Object keys are ALWAYS DISTINCT, so the equal-key case (the only case `<` and `<=` decide differently) is UNREACHABLE: for every (left,right) pair the comparator sees, left !== right, so `left < right` and `left <= right` return the identical result. Verified empirically by applying the mutation to the source and content-addressing rich nested objects with many keys (including '', ' ', 'a', 'A', 'aa') — every output is byte-identical. No test can distinguish the two.

The same key-sort comparator's second boundary `left > right` vs `left >= right`. Object keys are always distinct, so the equal-key case is unreachable and the two boundaries return the identical result for every pair the comparator sees. Verified empirically (byte-identical content addresses over rich nested objects). No test can distinguish the two.

The primitive-passthrough guard `typeof value === 'string'` mutated to `typeof value === ''`. With the guard always false for a string, a string `s` no longer takes the passthrough branch and instead falls through to `return String(value)`. But `String(s) === s` for every string, so canonicalizeForAddress returns the identical value either way, and the content address is unchanged. Verified empirically by applying the mutation to the source and content-addressing a bare string — the output is byte-identical to the original. (The `||` disjunction mutants on the same line are NOT equivalent and ARE killed by a real test addressing a number/boolean primitive.)

The DEFAULT VALUE of the `algo` parameter (`algo: 'sha256' | 'blake3' = 'sha256'`) mutated to `algo = ''`. addressedDigestOf branches only on `if (algo === 'blake3')`; every other value (including '' and 'sha256') falls to the sha256 branch. So calling addressedDigestOf(bytes) with no algo arg produces the IDENTICAL sha256 digest whether the default is 'sha256' or ''. Verified empirically by applying the mutation to the source and comparing — byte-identical integrity_digest and algo='sha256'. (A real test pins the genuine contract: the no-arg default ≡ 'sha256' ≠ 'blake3' — but that test cannot kill THIS mutant because '' also routes to sha256. Honestly equivalent.)

Wave-6 reactive survivor (S6.2); re-addressed for the Wave-6.5.1 generation-bounded dispatch rewrite (line 98→111, S6.1a). The DEFAULT EmissionPolicy sentinel `const EMIT_ALL = { kind: 'all' }` mutated to `{ kind: '' }`. The emission axis is decided ONLY by `policy.kind === 'distinct'` (in replay1's `emit` and fanout's `publish`); the `'all'` pole is never compared with `===` anywhere. `'' !== 'distinct'`, so an `EMIT_ALL` whose kind is '' takes the exact same no-dedup fan-out path as `'all'` for every default-policy caller (compositor/derived/timeline-reentrancy/etc.). Verified empirically: SURVIVES the full deterministic reactive covering suite. No test can distinguish {kind:'all'} from {kind:''} — the sentinel value is only ever the ABSENCE of 'distinct'.

Wave-6 reactive survivor (S6.2); re-addressed for the Wave-6.5.1 generation-bounded dispatch rewrite (line 216→284, S6.1a). The DEFAULT ReentrancyPolicy sentinel `reentrancy: ReentrancyPolicy = 'synchronous'` mutated to `= ''`. The reentrancy axis is decided ONLY by `reentrancy === 'deferred'` (in replay1's `publish`); the `'synchronous'` pole is never compared with `===`. `'' !== 'deferred'`, so a default of '' takes the identical depth-first synchronous nested fan-out path as 'synchronous' for every default-reentrancy caller (derived/timeline/compositor). Verified empirically: SURVIVES the full deterministic reactive covering suite. The sentinel value is only ever the ABSENCE of 'deferred'; no test can distinguish it.

Wave-6 reactive survivor (S6.2). `Signal.make`'s explicit EmissionPolicy sentinel `CellKernel.replay1(initial, { kind: 'all' }, 'deferred')` mutated to `{ kind: '' }`. The kernel decides emission ONLY by `policy.kind === 'distinct'`; `'' !== 'distinct'` routes to the identical no-dedup fan-out as `'all'`. A signal's value channel therefore delivers every publish unchanged. Verified empirically: SURVIVES the full deterministic reactive covering suite (242 tests). The sentinel value is only ever the ABSENCE of 'distinct'; no test can distinguish {kind:'all'} from {kind:''}.

Wave-6 reactive survivor (S6.2). `Signal.controllable`'s explicit EmissionPolicy sentinel `CellKernel.replay1(0, { kind: 'all' }, 'deferred')` mutated to `{ kind: '' }`. The kernel decides emission ONLY by `policy.kind === 'distinct'`; `'' !== 'distinct'` routes to the identical no-dedup fan-out as `'all'` (every seek publishes). Verified empirically: SURVIVES the full deterministic reactive covering suite (242 tests). The sentinel is only ever the absence of 'distinct'; no test can distinguish {kind:'all'} from {kind:''}.

Wave-6 reactive survivor (S6.2). `Signal.audio`'s explicit EmissionPolicy sentinel `CellKernel.replay1(0, { kind: 'all' }, 'deferred')` mutated to `{ kind: '' }`. The kernel decides emission ONLY by `policy.kind === 'distinct'`; `'' !== 'distinct'` routes to the identical no-dedup fan-out as `'all'` (every poll publishes). Verified empirically: SURVIVES the full deterministic reactive covering suite. NOTE (RESOLVED by S6.1a / Wave 6.5.1): during the S6.1 harness pass this mutant transiently registered a FALSE KILL because the `subscribe-during-publish` fixture + oracle were mid-rewrite and red on the clean tree independent of any mutant; the S6.1a semantic-law correction (dispatch-snapshot membership, `late=[5,6]`) restored a green-on-clean reactive-conformance/reactive-capture, and the deterministic per-file covering suites confirm genuine equivalence.

Wave-6 reactive survivor (S6.2 — the 'custom case' gap, reclassified). `setupListener`'s `case 'custom': return;` label mutated to `case '':`. The custom case body is a NO-OP (custom signals are driven externally via the push API — no listener is attached), and the switch has NO default. So when `source.type === 'custom'` the mutated `case ''` never matches, the source falls out of the switch, and setupListener returns undefined — byte-identical to the original explicit `return`. No listener is attached either way; nothing observable changes. Verified empirically: SURVIVES the full deterministic reactive covering suite (242 tests). Provably unkillable — a no-op case's label value cannot be observed. (The OTHER two 'custom'→'' mutants, in normalizeSource:74 and initialValueForSource:130, ARE killed by existing tests — only this no-op-case label is equivalent.)

Wave-6 reactive survivor (S6.2 — part of the 'play dt' gap, reclassified). `const dt = (now - lastTime) * direction` mutated to `(now - lastTime) / direction`. `direction` is typed `1 | -1`, initialized to `1`, and only ever toggled between `1` and `-1` by `reverse()`. For any x, `x * 1 === x / 1` and `x * -1 === x / -1` — dividing by a unit-magnitude value equals multiplying by it. So for EVERY reachable value of `direction` the two operators yield identical `dt`; no reachable state distinguishes them (the algebraic sibling of the content-address comparator equivalents, which rely on keys being distinct). Verified empirically: SURVIVES the full deterministic reactive covering suite (242 tests). NOTE: the ADJACENT `-`→`+` mutant at 119:25 is a GENUINE HOLE and is now killed by a new product test; only the `*`→`/` variant is equivalent.
